claims.claim: Mechanical abuse accounts for 18% of EV accidents in China.
claims.quote: Mechanical abuse accounts for 18% of EV accidents in China.

claims.claim: The solid electrolyte interface (SEI) large-scale decomposition is triggered when the cell temperature exceeds 130 °C.
claims.quote: The solid electrolyte interface (SEI) large-scale decomposition is triggered when the cell temperature exceeds 130 °C.

claims.claim: The anode granular layer becomes denser as the SOC increases, which leads to an increase in its stiffness and strength.
claims.quote: The anode granular layer becomes denser as the SOC increases, which leads to an increase in its stiffness and strength.

claims.claim: In the mechanical-electrical-thermal coupling model, when the distance between positive and negative current collectors is less than threshold r, a short-circuit resistor Rs is used to simulate the ISC; the higher the SOC, the sooner the ISC occurs, so r is set to increase as the SOC increases.
claims.quote: When the distance is less than a set threshold r, a short-circuit resistor Rs is used instead of the equivalent circuit to simulate the ISC. The higher the SOC, the sooner the ISC of battery, so r is set to increase as the SOC increases.

claims.claim: The simulation model cannot simulate the force-electrical-thermal response of the battery under axial compression due to model limitations.
claims.quote: due to the limitation of the simulation model, this model cannot simulate the force–electrical–thermal response of the battery under axial compression.

claims.claim: A broad-scale internal short circuit event can cause cell temperature to rapidly rise to 300°C or higher, leading to cathode material decomposition and massive oxygen release.
claims.quote: This broad-scale ISC event can cause the cell temperature to rapidly rise to 300°C or higher, leading to cathode material decomposition and massive oxygen release.

claims.claim: When cell pressure reaches its designed critical level, the safety valve activates, releasing gases and initiating the initial venting event.
claims.quote: Once the cell pressure reaches its designed critical level, the safety valve will activate, releasing gases and initiating the initial venting event.

claims.claim: Heat release rates during fire propagation at the system level can rapidly escalate to the MW scale, potentially igniting other combustible components and leading to catastrophic fire incidents.
claims.quote: The heat release rates (HRRs) during fire propagation at the system level can rapidly escalate to the MW scale, potentially igniting other combustible components and leading to catastrophic fire incidents.

claims.claim: Thermal runaway performance is influenced by materials, state of charge (SOC), and state of health (SOH), meaning thermodynamic parameters obtained from calorimetry tests are specific to one chemical system or state.
claims.quote: thermodynamic parameters obtained from calorimetry tests are specific to one chemical system or state, as TR performance is influenced by materials, state of charge (SOC), and state of health (SOH).

claims.claim: Chemical crosstalk between cathode oxygen and anode can directly generate heat and trigger thermal runaway without severe internal short circuit.
claims.quote: chemical crosstalk between cathode oxygen and anode can directly generate heat, triggering TR without severe ISC.

claims.claim: Even if an internal short circuit is induced, the joule heat from ISC may be limited by sharply increased battery resistance, thus contributing minimally to thermal runaway heat generation.
claims.quote: even if ISC is induced, the joule heat from ISC may be limited by sharply increased battery resistance, thus contributing minimally to TR heat generation.

claims.claim: When battery voltage exceeds 4.2 V during overcharging of lithium iron phosphate batteries, the temperature begins to change, and within a certain voltage range the temperature changes slowly.
claims.quote: Comparing the temperature at the center of the battery surface with the voltage between the two poles, when the voltage exceeds 4.2 V, the temperature begins to change, and within a certain voltage range, the temperature changes slowly.

claims.claim: When battery voltage exceeds 5.2 V during overcharging, the surface temperature begins to rise sharply, marking the transition to the early-mid stage of thermal runaway.
claims.quote: When the voltage is greater than 5.2 V, the temperature begins to rise sharply. When the voltage exceeds 5.2 V or the temperature exceeds 50 ◦C, it is defined as the early-mid stage of TR.

claims.claim: At higher charging rates during overcharging at 50% SOC, the slow temperature rise stage is shorter and thermal runaway occurs faster inside the battery.
claims.quote: the shorter the slow temperature rise stage, the faster TR occurs inside the battery, especially at higher charging rates.

claims.claim: Gas generation was detected during overcharging experiments on commercial lithium iron phosphate batteries when the battery surface temperature reached 53°C and the SOC reached 120%.
claims.quote: Fernandes et al. [19] conducted overcharging experiments on commercial lithium iron phosphate batteries and found that gas generation was detected when the battery surface temperature reached 53 ◦C and the battery State of Charge (SOC) reached 120 %.

claims.claim: Voltage-based thermal runaway detection is limited because when the voltage signal undergoes a sudden change, thermal runaway inside the battery has already occurred for some time.
claims.quote: when the voltage signal undergoes a sudden change, thermal runaway inside the battery has already occurred for some time, making it difficult to achieve very early warning based solely on a single voltage change.
